items.title: CMS issues interim final rule creating a nationwide Medicaid work-requirement framework
items.summary: CMS announced an interim final rule requiring certain Medicaid adults ages 19–64 to meet an 80-hours-per-month work requirement (including work, education, work programs, or community service) as a condition of eligibility. CMS says applicable states must implement no later than 2027-01-01, with exemptions including pregnancy, disability/medical frailty, certain caregivers, and American Indian/Alaska Native beneficiaries.

items.title: USDA SNAP technology-improvement grants (FY2026) accept applications through June 29
items.summary: USDA’s SNAP Process and Technology Improvement Grants (PTIG) program plans to award up to $5 million in FY 2026 funding for projects that modernize SNAP customer service, improve eligibility operations, or coordinate eligibility with other programs. USDA lists the Grants.gov close date as 2026-06-29, and the agency notes funded projects run through a three-year period ending 2029-09-30.

items.title: USDA finalizes larger WIC fruit-and-vegetable benefit amounts in WIC food package updates
items.summary: USDA’s WIC food package updates set new base monthly cash-value benefit (CVB) amounts of $24 for children, $43 for pregnant/postpartum participants, and $47 for breastfeeding participants (with annual inflation adjustments). USDA also notes that at the time of rule implementation, inflation-indexed amounts will continue at $26 (children), $47 (pregnant/postpartum), and $52 (breastfeeding) levels.

items.title: USDA publishes WIC income eligibility guidelines for July 2026–June 2027
items.summary: USDA published WIC income eligibility guidelines effective 2026-07-01 through 2027-06-30. For a 1-person household in the 48 contiguous states/D.C., the published amounts include $15,960 annually ($1,330 monthly) at 100% of the federal poverty guideline and $29,526 annually ($2,461 monthly) at 185% (reduced-price meals).

items.title: HUD issues updated guidance on eligibility timelines for the public-housing Asset Repositioning Fee
items.summary: HUD Notice PIH 2026-16 updates guidance for when public housing agencies can receive the Asset Repositioning Fee (ARF) tied to demolition/disposition and relocation activities. HUD describes ARF eligibility beginning on the first day of the next quarter after 6 months following the ARF trigger date, with ARF lasting 24 months for disposition and 36 months for demolition; the notice also references a 90-day minimum relocation notice standard.
items.source: U.S. Department of Housing and Urban Development

items.title: IRS posts updated details for the employer-provided child care credit starting in tax year 2026
items.summary: The IRS says that for amounts paid or incurred after 2025-12-31, the employer-provided child care credit equals 40% of qualified child care expenditures (50% for eligible small businesses) plus 10% of qualified child care resource and referral expenditures. The IRS directs filers to claim the credit using Form 8882.

items.title: Education Department posts 2026–27 federal student loan interest-rate period dates (effective July 1)
items.summary: Federal Student Aid posted information for the July 1, 2026 through June 30, 2027 period covering interest rate calculations for FFEL Program Stafford/PLUS/SLS and consolidation loans. Borrowers should verify which loan program they have and confirm their rate is tied to the 2026-07-01 to 2027-06-30 period when reviewing award letters.
items.source: Federal Student Aid (FSA Partners Knowledge Center)
